ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='final_result', args={'reasoning': 'This model transforms raw opportunity data into the target `Opportunity` schema. It handles various string normalizations, maps source stage names to predefined enum values, parses multiple date formats, and converts localized currency strings to a standard numeric format. A LEFT JOIN to `master_kunden` populates the `AccountId` based on the customer reference.', 'column_notes': [{'target_column': 'Id', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.opp_kennung'], 'explanation': 'Unique identifier for the opportunity, taken directly from the trimmed source `opp_kennung`.'}, {'target_column': 'Name', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.titel'], 'explanation': 'The name of the opportunity, mapped from the trimmed source `titel`.'}, {'explanation': "The current stage of the opportunity, mapped from the source `vertriebsphase` to one of the predefined enum values. Lowercasing and trimming are applied for robust matching, with 'Prospecting' as a default for unmapped values due to the NOT NULL constraint.", 'target_column': 'StageName', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.vertriebsphase']}, {'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.zieldatum'], 'target_column': 'CloseDate', 'explanation': "The expected close date of the opportunity, transformed from the source `zieldatum` (text) to 'YYYY-MM-DD' format. Multiple date formats are handled using a CASE statement and TO_DATE, with '2000-01-01' as a default for unparseable or NULL values due to the NOT NULL constraint."}, {'explanation': "The monetary value of the opportunity, cleaned by removing 'EUR ' prefixes and handling European vs. standard decimal/thousand separators before casting to DOUBLE PRECISION. NULL if the source `auftragswert` is empty or NULL.", 'target_column': 'Amount', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.auftragswert']}, {'target_column': 'CurrencyIsoCode', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.waehrungscode'], 'explanation': 'The currency code for the opportunity amount, taken from the trimmed source `waehrungscode`. Empty strings are converted to NULL.'}, {'explanation': 'The ID of the associated account, derived by joining `master_opportunities` on `kunden_ref` to `master_kunden` on `kundennummer`.', 'target_column': 'AccountId', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.kunden_ref', 'master_kunden.kundennummer']}, {'explanation': 'The legacy identifier for the opportunity, taken directly from the trimmed source `opp_kennung`.', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.opp_kennung'], 'target_column': 'Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c'}, {'source_columns': [], 'target_column': 'CreatedDate', 'explanation': 'Placeholder for creation date; set to NULL as no direct source mapping is available.'}, {'explanation': 'Placeholder for last modification date; set to NULL as no direct source mapping is available.', 'target_column': 'LastModifiedDate', 'source_columns': []}, {'explanation': 'Indicates if the record is deleted; defaulted to 0 as no source indicates deletion status.', 'source_columns': [], 'target_column': 'IsDeleted'}], 'dbt_sql': '
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunities_cleaned_raw_values AS (
    SELECT
        TRIM(opp_kennung) AS opp_kennung,
        TRIM(titel) AS titel,
        TRIM(vertriebsphase) AS vertriebsphase,
        TRIM(zieldatum) AS zieldatum,
        TRIM(auftragswert) AS auftragswert,
        NULLIF(TRIM(waehrungscode), '') AS waehrungscode,
        TRIM(kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }}
)
SELECT
    opp_kennung AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('won', 'closed won', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'gewonnen', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'closed lost', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('qualifikation', 'quali', 'qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for NOT NULL target column
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum -- YYYY-MM-DD
        WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE '2000-01-01' -- Default for NOT NULL target column if unparseable or NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR auftragswert = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE
            CAST(
                CASE
                    WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '^EUR\s*', '', 'i') LIKE '%.%,%' THEN REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '^EUR\s*', '', 'i'), '.', ''), ',', '.')
                    ELSE REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '^EUR\s*', '', 'i'), ',', '.')
                END
            AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    kunden.kundennummer AS "AccountId",
    opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::text AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::text AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunities_cleaned_raw_values AS o
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS kunden
    ON o.kunden_ref = kunden.kundennummer

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 17, 0, 11, 766251, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Target table: `Opportunity`.

ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='final_result', args={'reasoning': 'This model transforms raw opportunity data by standardizing stage names and date formats, cleaning numerical amounts, and linking to customer accounts. Default values are provided for NOT NULL target columns where source data might be missing or unparseable.', 'dbt_sql': '
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    o.opp_kennung AS "Id",
    COALESCE(o.titel, 'Unknown Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won', 'closedwon') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'closed lost', 'closedlost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('in kontakt', 'prospecting', 'prospect', 'prospection') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('qualifikation', 'quali', 'qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for unmapped or NULL values, as StageName is NOT NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        TO_DATE('1900-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-01') -- Default date for unparseable/NULL as CloseDate is NOT NULL. Changed to YYYY-MM-01 to avoid invalid day for 1900-01-01
    )::TEXT AS "CloseDate",
    CAST(
        REPLACE(
            CASE
                WHEN o.auftragswert IS NULL THEN NULL
                WHEN o.auftragswert LIKE '%,%' THEN REPLACE(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\s*', '', 'i')), '.', '')
                ELSE TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\s*', '', 'i'))
            END,
        ',', '.')
    AS DOUBLE PRECISION) AS "Amount",
    o.waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    k.kundennummer AS "AccountId", -- from master_kunden, joined via kunden_ref
    o.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS o
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS k
ON
    o.kunden_ref = k.kundennummer

Defaults to '1900-01-01' for unparseable or NULL values.", 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.zieldatum'], 'target_column': 'CloseDate'}, {'explanation': "Cleaned from 'auftragswert' by removing 'EUR ' prefix and handling European vs. American decimal/thousands separators before casting to DOUBLE PRECISION.", 'target_column': 'Amount', 'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.auftragswert']}, {'source_columns': ['master_opportunities.waehrungscode'], 'target_column': 'CurrencyIsoCode', 'explanation': 'Directly mapped from the currency code.'}, {'source_columns': ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a staff data engineer judging competing dbt models for the SAME target table.

You receive the source/target context and N candidate models (numbered from 0). Pick the single best candidate by:
1. correctness against the target schema (names, types, enums) — mixed-case columns must use double-quoted aliases
2. PostgreSQL validity (no TRY_CAST/SAFE_CAST/ISNULL/NVL or invented functions)
3. data preservation (no needless filtering or lossy casts)
4. clarity

Return a JudgeChoice with chosen_index and a 1-2 sentence justification.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 17, 0, 52, 270836, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Target table: `Opportunity`.

# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.

## Candidate 0
```sql

WITH opportunities_cleaned_raw_values AS (
    SELECT
        TRIM(opp_kennung) AS opp_kennung,
        TRIM(titel) AS titel,
        TRIM(vertriebsphase) AS vertriebsphase,
        TRIM(zieldatum) AS zieldatum,
        TRIM(auftragswert) AS auftragswert,
        NULLIF(TRIM(waehrungscode), '') AS waehrungscode,
        TRIM(kunden_ref) AS kunden_ref
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }}
)
SELECT
    opp_kennung AS "Id",
    titel AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('won', 'closed won', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'gewonnen', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'closed lost', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('qualifikation', 'quali', 'qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(vertriebsphase) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for NOT NULL target column
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN zieldatum -- YYYY-MM-DD
        WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE '2000-01-01' -- Default for NOT NULL target column if unparseable or NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN auftragswert IS NULL OR auftragswert = '' THEN NULL
        ELSE
            CAST(
                CASE
                    WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '^EUR\s*', '', 'i') LIKE '%.%,%' THEN REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '^EUR\s*', '', 'i'), '.', ''), ',', '.')
                    ELSE REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(auftragswert, '^EUR\s*', '', 'i'), ',', '.')
                END
            AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    kunden.kundennummer AS "AccountId",
    opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::text AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::text AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunities_cleaned_raw_values AS o
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS kunden
    ON o.kunden_ref = kunden.kundennummer
;
```

        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'closed lost', 'closedlost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('in kontakt', 'prospecting', 'prospect', 'prospection') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('qualifikation', 'quali', 'qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for unmapped or NULL values, as StageName is NOT NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')

            ELSE NULL

                WHEN o.auftragswert LIKE '%,%' THEN REPLACE(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\s*', '', 'i')), '.', '')
                ELSE TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\s*', '', 'i'))
            END,
        ',', '.')
    AS DOUBLE PRECISION) AS "Amount",
    o.waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    k.kundennummer AS "AccountId", -- from master_kunden, joined via kunden_ref
    o.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS o
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS k
ON
    o.kunden_ref = k.kundennummer

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.

```sql

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    o.opp_kennung AS "Id",
    COALESCE(o.titel, 'Unknown Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won', 'closedwon') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'closed lost', 'closedlost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('in kontakt', 'prospecting', 'prospect', 'prospection') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('qualifikation', 'quali', 'qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for unmapped or NULL values, as StageName is NOT NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        TO_DATE('1900-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-01') -- Default date for unparseable/NULL as CloseDate is NOT NULL. Changed to YYYY-MM-01 to avoid invalid day for 1900-01-01
    )::TEXT AS "CloseDate",
    CAST(
        REPLACE(
            CASE
                WHEN o.auftragswert IS NULL THEN NULL
                WHEN o.auftragswert LIKE '%,%' THEN REPLACE(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\s*', '', 'i')), '.', '')
                ELSE TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\s*', '', 'i'))
            END,
        ',', '.')
    AS DOUBLE PRECISION) AS "Amount",
    o.waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    k.kundennummer AS "AccountId", -- from master_kunden, joined via kunden_ref
    o.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS o
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS k
ON
    o.kunden_ref = k.kundennummer

```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.

- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.

## query_postgres tool rules

- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 17, 1, 22, 43283, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Your previous dbt model for `Contact` failed.

Previous SQL:
```sql

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    TRIM(mk.kontakt_id) AS "Id",
    TRIM(mk.rufname) AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(TRIM(mk.familienname), 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    LOWER(TRIM(mk.kontakt_email)) AS "Email",
    TRIM(mk.tel) AS "Phone",
    TRIM(mk.berufsbezeichnung) AS "Title",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(mk.rolle) IN ('Entscheider', 'Decision Maker') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN TRIM(mk.rolle) = 'End User' THEN 'End User'
        WHEN TRIM(mk.rolle) = 'Technical Contact' THEN 'Technical Contact'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mk.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('de', 'deutsch') THEN 'DE'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mk.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('en', 'englisch', 'english') THEN 'EN'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(mk.korrespondenzsprache)) = 'fr' THEN 'FR'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    mkd.kundennummer AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(mk.kontakt_id) AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    NULL::text AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::text AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kontakte') }} AS mk
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS mkd
    ON TRIM(mk.kd_nummer) = TRIM(mkd.kundennummer)

```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 17, 1, 22, 43128, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Your previous dbt model for `Opportunity` failed.

Previous SQL:
```sql

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    o.opp_kennung AS "Id",
    COALESCE(o.titel, 'Unknown Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won', 'closedwon') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'closed lost', 'closedlost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('in kontakt', 'prospecting', 'prospect', 'prospection') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('qualifikation', 'quali', 'qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for unmapped or NULL values, as StageName is NOT NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        TO_DATE('1900-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-01') -- Default date for unparseable/NULL as CloseDate is NOT NULL. Changed to YYYY-MM-01 to avoid invalid day for 1900-01-01
    )::TEXT AS "CloseDate",
    CAST(
        REPLACE(
            CASE
                WHEN o.auftragswert IS NULL THEN NULL
                WHEN o.auftragswert LIKE '%,%' THEN REPLACE(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\s*', '', 'i')), '.', '')
                ELSE TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\s*', '', 'i'))
            END,
        ',', '.')
    AS DOUBLE PRECISION) AS "Amount",
    o.waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    k.kundennummer AS "AccountId", -- from master_kunden, joined via kunden_ref
    o.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS o
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS k
ON
    o.kunden_ref = k.kundennummer

```

ModelResponse(parts=[ToolCallPart(tool_name='final_result', args={'dbt_sql': ''''{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    o.opp_kennung AS "Id",
    COALESCE(o.titel, 'Unknown Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won', 'closedwon') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'closed lost', 'closedlost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('in kontakt', 'prospecting', 'prospect', 'prospection') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('qualifikation', 'quali', 'qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for unmapped or NULL values, as StageName is NOT NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        TO_DATE('1900-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') -- Default date for unparseable/NULL as CloseDate is NOT NULL.
    )::TEXT AS "CloseDate",
    CAST(
        CASE
            WHEN o.auftragswert IS NULL THEN NULL
            ELSE
                -- First, clean up 'EUR' and surrounding spaces
                CASE
                    WHEN (TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\\s*', '', 'i'))) ~ '^\\s*$' THEN NULL -- Handle empty string after cleaning
                    -- Define a common cleaned string for repeated use
                    WHEN TRUE THEN
                        (SELECT cleaned_val FROM (
                            SELECT TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\\s*', '', 'i')) AS cleaned_val
                        ) AS _tmp)
                    ELSE NULL
                END
        END
    AS TEXT) AS cleaned_amount_str,
    CAST(
        CASE
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str IS NULL OR cleaned_amount_str = '' THEN NULL
            -- European format with thousand separator and comma decimal (e.g., 1.234.567,89)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d{1,2}$' THEN
                REPLACE(REPLACE(cleaned_amount_str, '.', ''), ',', '.')
            -- US format with comma thousand separator and dot decimal (e.g., 1,234,567.89)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d{1,3}(,\d{3})*\.\d{1,2}$' THEN
                REPLACE(cleaned_amount_str, ',', '')
            -- European format with only comma decimal (e.g., 1234,56)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d+,\d{1,2}$' THEN
                REPLACE(cleaned_amount_str, ',', '.')
            -- US format with only dot decimal or integer (e.g., 1234.56 or 1234)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d+(\.\d{1,2})?$' THEN
                cleaned_amount_str
            ELSE NULL -- Unrecognized format, return NULL
        END
    AS DOUBLE PRECISION) AS "Amount",
    o.waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    k.kundennummer AS "AccountId", -- from master_kunden, joined via kunden_ref
    o.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS o
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS k
ON
    o.kunden_ref = k.kundennummer'''', 'reasoning': 'The primary fix addresses the "invalid input syntax for type double precision" error by implementing a robust parsing logic for the "Amount" column. This logic systematically cleans currency symbols and handles diverse European and US numeric string formats before casting to DOUBLE PRECISION, returning NULL for any unparseable values. Other transformations remain consistent with target specifications and PostgreSQL syntax rules.', 'column_notes': [{'target_column': 'Id', 'explanation': 'Directly mapped from the opportunity ID.', 'source_columns': ['o.opp_kennung']}, {'target_column': 'Name', 'explanation': "Mapped from the opportunity title, with 'Unknown Opportunity' as a fallback for NULLs to satisfy NOT NULL constraint.", 'source_columns': ['o.titel']}, {'explanation': "Mapped from the sales stage, normalized to target enum values. Defaults to 'Prospecting' for unmapped or NULL values.", 'target_column': 'StageName', 'source_columns': ['o.vertriebsphase']}, {'target_column': 'CloseDate', 'explanation': "Parsed from various date formats in the source, converted to YYYY-MM-DD text. Defaults to '1900-01-01' for unparseable or NULL values to satisfy NOT NULL constraint.", 'source_columns': ['o.zieldatum']}, {'source_columns': ['o.auftragswert'], 'target_column': 'Amount', 'explanation': "Cleaned by removing 'EUR' and spaces, then converting various European and US numeric string formats (with and without thousand separators) to a standard decimal string, and finally casting to DOUBLE PRECISION. Invalid formats result in NULL."}, {'source_columns': ['o.waehrungscode'], 'target_column': 'CurrencyIsoCode', 'explanation': 'Directly mapped from the currency code.'}, {'target_column': 'AccountId', 'explanation': 'Mapped from the customer number via a join with master_kunden.', 'source_columns': ['k.kundennummer', 'o.kunden_ref']}, {'explanation': 'Mapped from the opportunity ID as a legacy identifier.', 'target_column': 'Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c', 'source_columns': ['o.opp_kennung']}, {'target_column': 'CreatedDate', 'explanation': 'Set to NULL as no source mapping is available.', 'source_columns': []}, {'source_columns': [], 'target_column': 'LastModifiedDate', 'explanation': 'Set to NULL as no source mapping is available.'}, {'source_columns': [], 'explanation': 'Set to 0 as a default value.', 'target_column': 'IsDeleted'}], 'target_table': 'Opportunity'}, tool_call_id='pyd_ai_a230fa6a0c704079af8ed574cbff3a97', provider_name='google-cloud', provider_details={'thought_signature': ''})], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=7613, output_tokens=5004, details={'thoughts_tokens': 3445, 'text_prompt_tokens': 7613, 'text_candidates_tokens': 1559}), model_name='gemini-2.5-flash', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 17, 2, 12, 384189, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='google-cloud', provider_url='https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'STOP', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 17, 1, 45, 105321, tzinfo=TzInfo(0)), 'traffic_type': 'ON_DEMAND'}, provider_response_id='eVNBaum2BqLFmLAPwdfTiAk', finish_reason='stop')

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 17, 2, 22, 634255, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Your previous dbt model for `Opportunity` failed.

Previous SQL:
```sql
'''{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    o.opp_kennung AS "Id",
    COALESCE(o.titel, 'Unknown Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won', 'closedwon') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'closed lost', 'closedlost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('in kontakt', 'prospecting', 'prospect', 'prospection') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('qualifikation', 'quali', 'qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for unmapped or NULL values, as StageName is NOT NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        TO_DATE('1900-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') -- Default date for unparseable/NULL as CloseDate is NOT NULL.
    )::TEXT AS "CloseDate",
    CAST(
        CASE
            WHEN o.auftragswert IS NULL THEN NULL
            ELSE
                -- First, clean up 'EUR' and surrounding spaces
                CASE
                    WHEN (TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\\s*', '', 'i'))) ~ '^\\s*$' THEN NULL -- Handle empty string after cleaning
                    -- Define a common cleaned string for repeated use
                    WHEN TRUE THEN
                        (SELECT cleaned_val FROM (
                            SELECT TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\\s*', '', 'i')) AS cleaned_val
                        ) AS _tmp)
                    ELSE NULL
                END
        END
    AS TEXT) AS cleaned_amount_str,
    CAST(
        CASE
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str IS NULL OR cleaned_amount_str = '' THEN NULL
            -- European format with thousand separator and comma decimal (e.g., 1.234.567,89)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d{1,2}$' THEN
                REPLACE(REPLACE(cleaned_amount_str, '.', ''), ',', '.')
            -- US format with comma thousand separator and dot decimal (e.g., 1,234,567.89)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d{1,3}(,\d{3})*\.\d{1,2}$' THEN
                REPLACE(cleaned_amount_str, ',', '')
            -- European format with only comma decimal (e.g., 1234,56)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d+,\d{1,2}$' THEN
                REPLACE(cleaned_amount_str, ',', '.')
            -- US format with only dot decimal or integer (e.g., 1234.56 or 1234)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d+(\.\d{1,2})?$' THEN
                cleaned_amount_str
            ELSE NULL -- Unrecognized format, return NULL
        END
    AS DOUBLE PRECISION) AS "Amount",
    o.waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    k.kundennummer AS "AccountId", -- from master_kunden, joined via kunden_ref
    o.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS o
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS k
ON
    o.kunden_ref = k.kundennummer'''
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 17, 2, 22, 634255, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Your previous dbt model for `Opportunity` failed.

Previous SQL:
```sql
'''{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    o.opp_kennung AS "Id",
    COALESCE(o.titel, 'Unknown Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won', 'closedwon') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'closed lost', 'closedlost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('in kontakt', 'prospecting', 'prospect', 'prospection') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) IN ('qualifikation', 'quali', 'qualification') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(o.vertriebsphase) = 'in prüfung' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for unmapped or NULL values, as StageName is NOT NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')
            WHEN o.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        TO_DATE('1900-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') -- Default date for unparseable/NULL as CloseDate is NOT NULL.
    )::TEXT AS "CloseDate",
    CAST(
        CASE
            WHEN o.auftragswert IS NULL THEN NULL
            ELSE
                -- First, clean up 'EUR' and surrounding spaces
                CASE
                    WHEN (TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\\s*', '', 'i'))) ~ '^\\s*$' THEN NULL -- Handle empty string after cleaning
                    -- Define a common cleaned string for repeated use
                    WHEN TRUE THEN
                        (SELECT cleaned_val FROM (
                            SELECT TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, 'EUR\\s*', '', 'i')) AS cleaned_val
                        ) AS _tmp)
                    ELSE NULL
                END
        END
    AS TEXT) AS cleaned_amount_str,
    CAST(
        CASE
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str IS NULL OR cleaned_amount_str = '' THEN NULL
            -- European format with thousand separator and comma decimal (e.g., 1.234.567,89)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d{1,2}$' THEN
                REPLACE(REPLACE(cleaned_amount_str, '.', ''), ',', '.')
            -- US format with comma thousand separator and dot decimal (e.g., 1,234,567.89)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d{1,3}(,\d{3})*\.\d{1,2}$' THEN
                REPLACE(cleaned_amount_str, ',', '')
            -- European format with only comma decimal (e.g., 1234,56)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d+,\d{1,2}$' THEN
                REPLACE(cleaned_amount_str, ',', '.')
            -- US format with only dot decimal or integer (e.g., 1234.56 or 1234)
            WHEN cleaned_amount_str ~ '^-?\d+(\.\d{1,2})?$' THEN
                cleaned_amount_str
            ELSE NULL -- Unrecognized format, return NULL
        END
    AS DOUBLE PRECISION) AS "Amount",
    o.waehrungscode AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    k.kundennummer AS "AccountId", -- from master_kunden, joined via kunden_ref
    o.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_opportunities') }} AS o
LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('fixture_master_src', 'master_kunden') }} AS k
ON
    o.kunden_ref = k.kundennummer'''
```

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing dbt SQL.

You receive a description of a source database (schemas, columns, optionally sample rows) and a target table specification. Your job is to write ONE dbt model — a SELECT statement that transforms the source data into the target schema.

Return a structured TableMapping with:
- target_table: name of the model
- dbt_sql: full SELECT statement
- column_notes: per-target-column source + brief explanation
- reasoning: 1-3 sentences justifying your choices

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable.
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`).
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 6, 28, 17, 3, 14, 707343, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='Your previous dbt model for `Account` failed.

        WHEN LOWER(kunden.kundenklasse) = 'gold' THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN LOWER(kunden.kundenklasse) = 'silver' THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN LOWER(kunden.kundenklasse) = 'bronze' THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN LOWER(kunden.kundenklasse) IN ('platinum', 'platin') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    TRIM(kunden.vertriebsgebiet) AS "Region__c",
    TRIM(kunden.industrie) AS "Industry",
    TRIM(kunden.homepage) AS "Website",
    TRIM(kunden.stadt) AS "BillingCity",
    TRIM(kunden.land_region) AS "BillingCountry",
    kunden.kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL::text AS "CreatedDate",

- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    mo.opp_kennung AS "Id",
    COALESCE(mo.titel, mo.opp_kennung) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(mo.vertriebsphase) = 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(mo.vertriebsphase) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(mo.vertriebsphase) IN ('qualification', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(mo.vertriebsphase) IN ('lost', 'verloren') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN LOWER(mo.vertriebsphase) = 'in kontakt' THEN 'Perception Analysis' -- Best guess for "In Contact"
        ELSE 'Prospecting' -- Default for NOT NULL target
    END AS "StageName",
    COALESCE(
        CASE
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN mo.zieldatum
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            WHEN mo.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(mo.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ELSE NULL
        END,
        CURRENT_DATE::TEXT -- Fallback for unparseable/N/A dates for a NOT NULL target
    ) AS "CloseDate",
    CAST(
        CASE
            WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(mo.auftragswert, '[A-Za-z€$ ]+', ''), '^.*,\d{2}$' , 'x') = 'x' -- Detects European format: "1.234,56"
                THEN REGEXP_REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(mo.auftragswert, '[A-Za-z€$ ]+', ''), '\.', '')
            ELSE REGEXP_REPLACE(mo.auftragswert, '[A-Za-z€$ ]+', '')
        END AS DOUBLE PRECISION
    ) AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN mo.waehrungscode = '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN LOWER(mo.waehrungscode) = 'dollar' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN mo.waehrungscode IN ('CHF', 'USD') THEN mo.waehrungscode
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    mo.kunden_ref AS "AccountId",
    mo.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM

        WHEN LOWER(projektstatus) IN ('inaktiv', 'inactive') THEN 'Cancelled'
        WHEN LOWER(projektstatus) IN ('in bearbeitung', 'pending') THEN 'In Planning'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CAST(
        COALESCE(
            -- Format: YYYY-MM-DD, explicitly excluding '0000-00-00' which causes a PostgreSQL error during direct casting
            CASE
                WHEN go_live_datum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' AND go_live_datum != '0000-00-00'
                THEN CAST(go_live_datum AS DATE)
            END,
            -- Format: YYYYMMDD
            CASE
                WHEN go_live_datum ~ '^\d{8}$'
                THEN TO_DATE(go_live_datum, 'YYYYMMDD')

            -- Format: MM/DD/YYYY

                WHEN go_live_datum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$'

            END,

    kunden_kennung AS "Account__c",
